selected_plan.title: Add Korean localization to WhatIfInvest
selected_plan.summary: Use system-language-driven localization resources plus a small central string layer so the current WhatIfInvest experience reads naturally in English and Korean.

retained_alternatives.title: Add an in-app language switcher
retained_alternatives.summary: Expose an app preference for English and Korean inside the product UI.
pros: Avoids dependence on system language settings
cons: Widens scope into settings UX and preference persistence
Not required by the current request

retained_alternatives.title: Inline locale checks per view
retained_alternatives.summary: Translate strings by branching on locale directly inside each SwiftUI view.
pros: Fast for one or two strings
cons: Easy to miss dynamic strings and trust copy

leaf_tasks.title: Add English and Korean localization resources
leaf_tasks.description: Create the shared localization tables and launch-screen resources needed for English fallback and Korean UI copy.
leaf_tasks.why: The app needs a durable translation source instead of scattered literals.
done_criteria: English and Korean string resources exist for the app surface
The project recognizes Korean as a supported localization

verification.description: Inspect the resource tables and project settings to confirm both locales are wired correctly.

leaf_tasks.title: Route UI and dynamic copy through a central localizer
leaf_tasks.description: Replace hard-coded English strings across views, formatting helpers, model messages, and error copy with localized accessors and formatters.
leaf_tasks.why: A partial localization pass would leave the Korean experience visibly incomplete.
done_criteria: Visible UI labels and trust copy are localized
Dynamic summary and validation strings are localized

verification.description: Review every user-facing string path in the app to confirm it now resolves through localized resources.

leaf_tasks.title: Verify the localized app still builds and preserves English fallback
leaf_tasks.description: Build the app and update focused tests where the new localization structure changes behavior or selectors.
leaf_tasks.why: Localization changes touch many text surfaces and can easily break identifiers or project resources.
done_criteria: Existing English-path tests still make sense
Core localized surfaces are covered by verification

verification.description: Build the WhatIfInvest scheme after localization changes.
Run focused app tests that exercise the localized flow.

risk_summary.description: Some dynamic or less obvious strings could remain in English if the pass is not exhaustive.
risk_summary.mitigation: Centralize formatted strings and re-scan the codebase for user-facing copy.

risk_summary.description: UI tests that rely on visible English copy can break once localization is introduced.
risk_summary.mitigation: Prefer accessibility identifiers where possible and keep English fallback intact for the default test path.
